brief_document.title: Enforce single-writer discipline at the type level
brief_document.purpose: Invariant #3 (single writer per workflow) is currently enforced by documentation and code review alone. EventStore::append is public, so any caller can bypass Recorder and append directly. This brief splits the trait into a read-only public surface and a write-only crate-internal surface, making the single-writer constraint a compile-time guarantee rather than a social contract.
brief_document.task: Split the EventStore trait into ReadableEventStore (public, read-only methods) and WritableEventStore (append, crate-internal). Introduce a sealed capability token that only Recorder can construct, gating WritableEventStore access. Migrate all non-Recorder call sites to ReadableEventStore. Retain SequenceConflict detection as runtime defense-in-depth.

requirements.title: Split EventStore into ReadableEventStore and WritableEventStore
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a ReadableEventStore trait containing all read-only methods (read_history, query, get_latest_sequence, etc.) and a WritableEventStore trait containing append. ReadableEventStore SHALL be public. WritableEventStore SHALL be crate-internal (pub(crate)) so that only code within aion-store can implement or call it directly.
acceptance: ReadableEventStore is a public trait in aion-store exposing all read-only EventStore methods.
WritableEventStore is a pub(crate) trait in aion-store exposing append.
Both traits have blanket or supertrait relationships so that a type implementing both can be used wherever either is required.
The original EventStore trait is removed or becomes a convenience alias/supertrait of both, not directly implemented by consumers.

requirements.title: Recorder gets exclusive WritableEventStore access via a sealed capability token
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a sealed newtype (WriteToken) that only Recorder can construct. WritableEventStore::append SHALL require a WriteToken parameter (or WritableEventStore SHALL only be obtainable through a sealed accessor that Recorder holds), so that external code cannot call append even if it has an Arc<dyn EventStore>.
acceptance: A WriteToken (or equivalent sealed capability) exists in a private module that Recorder can reach but external crates cannot construct.
Calling append without the token is a compile error, not a runtime error.
Recorder constructs the token internally; no public API exposes the token constructor.
A doc comment on the token explains the single-writer invariant it enforces.

requirements.title: Migrate existing code to ReadableEventStore where only reads are needed
requirements.spec: WHEN code outside the Recorder requires event store access, THE SYSTEM SHALL accept ReadableEventStore (not the full EventStore or WritableEventStore). THE SYSTEM SHALL update all function signatures, trait bounds, and Arc types that currently accept EventStore but only use read methods.
acceptance: Engine, lifecycle transitions, query handlers, and visibility projection accept Arc<dyn ReadableEventStore> (or equivalent) where they only read.
The conformance suite in aion-store tests both ReadableEventStore and WritableEventStore methods.
The libSQL store implementation implements both traits.
No non-Recorder code path holds a WritableEventStore reference or calls append.

requirements.title: SequenceConflict retained as runtime defense-in-depth
requirements.spec: EVEN WHEN the type-level split prevents external append calls at compile time, THE SYSTEM SHALL retain SequenceConflict detection in WritableEventStore::append so that any future bypass (e.g. via unsafe or internal misuse) is caught at runtime.
acceptance: The SequenceConflict error variant remains in StoreError and is returned when a sequence number collision is detected on append.
The conformance suite includes a test that triggers SequenceConflict to prove the runtime guard still works.
The error message on SequenceConflict explicitly names it as a double-writer bug indicator.
No SequenceConflict-related logic is removed or weakened by the trait split.

boundaries: SHALL splitting EventStore into ReadableEventStore and WritableEventStore traits
SHALL sealed capability token for write access
SHALL migrating all non-Recorder call sites to ReadableEventStore
SHALL retaining SequenceConflict as runtime defense-in-depth
SHALL updating the conformance suite and libSQL store implementation
SHALL NOT changing the Recorder's internal append logic or batching strategy
SHALL NOT distributed locking or multi-node single-writer enforcement
SHALL NOT visibility store trait splitting (separate concern)
SHALL NOT wire-protocol or server-layer changes
verification: cargo test -p aion-store passes, including conformance tests for both ReadableEventStore and WritableEventStore.
cargo test -p aion-store-libsql passes, proving libSQL implements both traits.
cargo test -p aion passes, proving Recorder still appends through WritableEventStore.
A compile-fail test (or reviewer verification) confirms that code outside aion-store/Recorder cannot call append.
Review that no non-Recorder code path holds a WritableEventStore reference.
Confirm WriteToken constructor is unreachable from external crates.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in the crate.
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] bypasses per CLAUDE.md. Tests needing a remote primary use a runtime env-gate, not #[ignore].
lib.rs is declarations and re-exports only; logic lives in named modules.
500-line file limit (excluding tests/comments/whitespace). The EventStore impl is split across append, read, and timer modules.
Depends only on aion-store (and transitively aion-core) among Aion crates — no dependency on the engine, beamr, or any other backend. Structural; must hold.
Raw SQL is permitted here because this crate IS a storage backend (the storage layer). No other crate issues SQL against this store; they go through the EventStore trait.
WorkflowStatus is derived from event history via aion-core's projection. No mutable stored status field; a cached projection must never diverge from the events.
LibSqlStore must pass the shared AC-007 conformance suite unmodified — the same behavioural standard as the oracle.
append's sequence guard and event inserts execute in a single libSQL transaction; a mismatch writes nothing and returns SequenceConflict — no partial batch is ever persisted.
LibSqlConfig is Deserialize-only and carries no hardcoded durability/WAL defaults; tunable values are surfaced for the operator to set, per CLAUDE.md no-assumed-defaults.
All libsql and serde errors are mapped into the StoreError taxonomy at the crate boundary; no foreign error type leaks across the EventStore surface.

resolved_context.intention: When an engine operator embeds Aion and writes no storage configuration, this is the store they get: a single file on disk, zero external infrastructure, full durability. The same crate serves the distributed path — the local file becomes an embedded replica syncing to a remote primary — without the operator learning a second backend. The in-memory reference store (cluster AC) proves the contract but forgets on restart; LibSqlStore makes durability the easy default, held to the identical behavioural standard as the oracle. A workflow that sleeps for months and resumes needs its history to survive every crash and reboot in between. When this cluster is done, an operator writes LibSqlStore::open("app.db") and has a production-grade durable event store the engine cannot tell apart from the oracle except that it remembers; the shared AC-007 conformance suite passes against it unchanged, plus a durability-specific suite proving state survives close-and-reopen.
